topic: Area of Study 3: Musical Traditions of Ireland
subject: music

items.correct: Rapid alternation between the written note and the note a step above.
items.why: Students confuse trill (rapid alternation between two notes) with vibrato (pitch oscillation around one note). Trills add ornamentation; vibrato adds expressive width to a sustained pitch.

items.correct: A recurring main theme (A) alternates with contrasting episodes (B, C, etc.).
items.misconception: A round where voices enter at different times.
items.why: Students confuse rondo (alternating refrain) with round (canon). Rondo: ABACA, ABACABA, etc. The A theme keeps coming back, like a refrain.

items.correct: A cadence ending on chord V, creating a sense of incompleteness.
items.misconception: A cadence that sounds wrong or is performed badly.
items.random_wrong: A cadence using only major chords.
items.why: Students think 'imperfect' means flawed. It's a technical term for an unfinished-sounding cadence - any chord moving to V leaves the music suspended, awaiting resolution.
It's Vivaldi. “Bach” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Continuous Baroque bass line is called?
items.why: It's Basso continuo. “Cadenza” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Exact answer: 1685. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
